workflow_rationale: Single-service feature (CLI output formatting) with clear scope and well-defined patterns already present in codebase. No multi-service dependencies or complex orchestration needed.

phases.name: CLI Enhancement
phases.description: Add verbose to format choices and integrate config default_report_format into CLI

subtasks.description: Add verbose to format choices in CLI

verification.command: python -c "from src.config import load_config; c = load_config(); print(c.default_report_format)"

subtasks.notes: Successfully integrated config default_report_format into run_analyze. Added --report-format CLI argument, load_config() call, and passing output_format to ReportGenerator. Also fixed a bug in report_generator.py where _generate_default_output was missing the cross_card_patterns parameter.

phases.name: Report Generator Enhancement
phases.description: Add VERBOSE_TEMPLATE with explanatory section headers and enforce 500-word limit on compact mode

verification.command: python -c "from src.report_generator import ReportGenerator, VERBOSE_TEMPLATE; print('VERBOSE_TEMPLATE' in dir())"

subtasks.notes: Added VERBOSE_TEMPLATE with explanatory headers for each section and _generate_verbose_output method. Updated valid_formats to include 'verbose'.

subtasks.description: Implement verbose output format in generate()

verification.command: python -c "from src.report_generator import ReportGenerator; from src.types import AnalysisResult; g = ReportGenerator(); r = g.generate(AnalysisResult(diagnosis='Test'), output_format='verbose'); print('# Verbose' in r or '# Análise' in r)"

subtasks.notes: Verbose output format is already implemented in generate() method via _generate_verbose_output(). The generate() method routes to _generate_verbose_output() when output_format='verbose', using VERBOSE_TEMPLATE with section headers. Verification confirmed all expected patterns present (Relatório, Seção, Sobre, Detalhado).

verification.command: python -c "from src.report_generator import ReportGenerator; from src.types import AnalysisResult; g = ReportGenerator(); r = g.generate(AnalysisResult(diagnosis='Test'), output_format='compact'); words = len(r.split()); print(f'Word count: {words}'); assert words <= 500, f'Too many words: {words}'"

phases.name: Tests
phases.description: Add tests for verbose and compact output modes

subtasks.description: Add CLI tests for verbose and compact format options

verification.command: python -m pytest tests/test_cli.py -v -k "verbose or compact" 2>&1 | head -30

subtasks.description: Add ReportGenerator tests for verbose format
Add test for compact mode 500-word limit

acceptance_criteria: verbose and compact modes documented in --help

verification_steps.name: CLI Help Check
verification_steps.expected_outcome: verbose and compact modes shown in help

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk change requires unit and integration test coverage

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend only on existing patterns, no file conflicts
